texto: If you want to know what love really is, take a look around you. ...It's giving life that counts. Until you're ready for it, all the rest is just a big fraud. All the crazy haircuts in the world won't keep it turning. Life isn't a 'love in;' it's the dishes and the orthodontist and the shoe repairman and... ground round instead of roast beef. And I'll tell you something else: it isn't going to a bed with a man that proves you're in love with him; it's getting up in the morning and facing the drab, miserable, wonderful everyday world with him that counts.
explicacao: Citação de Frank Beardsley sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “If you want to know what love really is, take a look around you. ...It's giving life that counts.…” articula amizade, love, sacrifice em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como bom-dia e love.
